beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, light breakfast at home
beats.reasoning: Monday morning start with full energy; Raj is a homebody who values a calm morning routine before heading out.
Commute from Greenwood to Kirkland workplace; Light Rail closure doesn't affect driving route; sunny weather makes for pleasant drive.

beats.activity: Prep work and morning service at fine-dining restaurant; Lunch service at restaurant
beats.reasoning: Chef de partie begins morning prep; lunch service starts; the 11am FIFA match is happening but Raj is focused on work and not attending.
Brief staff break during service lull; Raj grabs something at the restaurant before evening prep.

beats.activity: Prep for dinner service; Dinner service at restaurant
beats.reasoning: Afternoon mise en place and prep work; getting ready for busy dinner service on a sunny Monday evening.
Post-service drive back to Greenwood; late evening, light traffic; Raj heads straight home after a full shift.

beats.activity: Wind down with home brew and a book
beats.reasoning: Relaxes at home with a hobby he loves—home brewing or reading—before sleep; true to his homebody nature and laid-back personality.
Full night's rest after a long day of service; sleep spans overnight into Tuesday morning as required.

diary:
Started the day early with my usual routine—coffee in the kitchen, eggs on toast, nothing fancy. The drive to Kirkland was smooth; I caught the tail end of the morning news talking about the World Cup match later. Part of me thought about the downtown bars filling up at 11am, but that's not my scene anymore.

Service was solid today. Lunch was steady, nothing wild. During my break I grabbed some leftover halibut and just sat in the back, listening to the kitchen hum. Dinner service picked up around 6—a party of eight celebrating an anniversary, and I made sure their coq au vin was perfect. Those moments, when you nail something and the guests feel it, that's why I do this.

Drove home as the sun was still high. Poured myself a homebrew, cracked open my book. Felt good to decompress.
thought_process: It was a straightforward Monday, and honestly that suited me fine. The World Cup buzz downtown didn't pull at me—I'm more interested in what's happening in my kitchen than what's on a screen. The light rail closure didn't affect me since I drive to Kirkland anyway. My mood stayed even throughout; I was focused during service, attentive to the details that matter. The anniversary party energized me a bit—reminded me why precision and care in cooking still feel essential at 48. By evening I was ready to be alone with my thoughts and a good book.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
